blockingIssues.description: Sync Beacon gist reads use `gh gist view <id> --raw` without selecting `vibogit-beacon.json`, so multi-file gists can return concatenated payloads and break JSON parsing instead of reliably reading the beacon file.
Sync Beacon gist updates are not robust for existing multi-file gists because the edit flow does not safely target/update the beacon file with a guaranteed read-modify-write preservation path.
The settings UI switches to a read-only created-gist display once a gist ID exists, removing the required persistent input for joining another beacon by gist ID.
Local-machine exclusion in the remote machines panel depended on frontend/backend machine-name parity and could show the local machine as remote when identifiers diverged.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the tauri-fullstack skill to distinguish backend-only or plumbing-only changes from UI work so browser verification is optional when the feature contract is fully covered by cargo tests/typecheck and no user-surface behavior changed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Shared-state observations from sync-beacon-backend, fix-beacon-machine-name-and-commit-metadata, and fix-beacon-commit-metadata-pipeline all noted that mandatory browser verification did not fit backend/data-plumbing work and led to avoidable procedure deviations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in tauri-fullstack that test-first Rust changes are not mandatory for minimal pass-through or follow-up plumbing fixes when existing automated coverage already exercises the behavior.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fix-beacon-commit-metadata-pipeline review noted that the current skill over-prescribes TDD for a small pass-through metadata fix already covered by the existing suite.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document Sync Beacon multi-file gist file-targeting rules in shared mission knowledge immediately.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because the current milestone failed on open blocking product issues; codifying implementation guidance before the fix lands risks documenting broken behavior.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document machine-name parity requirements for Sync Beacon self-filtering in shared knowledge immediately.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because the underlying issue was already fixed in a follow-up feature and does not require an additional library entry right now.
